acne.duration: Mild acne: 1–2 weeks per breakout. Severe acne: 3–6 months with treatment.
precautions: Wash face twice daily with a gentle, non-comedogenic cleanser
Avoid touching your face with unwashed hands
Do not pop or squeeze pimples
Use oil-free, non-comedogenic moisturizers and sunscreen
Change pillowcases every 2–3 days
Stay hydrated and maintain a balanced diet
Avoid heavy makeup or remove it before sleeping
acne.tips: Apply benzoyl peroxide or salicylic acid products as spot treatments. Consult a dermatologist if over-the-counter treatments don't work within 6–8 weeks.

eczema.duration: Chronic condition with flare-ups lasting days to weeks. With treatment, flares can be managed to 1–2 weeks.
precautions: Moisturize skin at least twice daily with thick creams or ointments
Avoid known triggers such as certain soaps, fabrics, or allergens
Use mild, fragrance-free soaps and detergents
Wear loose-fitting, soft cotton clothing
Keep nails short to minimize skin damage from scratching
Maintain a cool, humid indoor environment
Avoid extreme temperatures and sudden temperature changes
eczema.tips: Apply moisturizer immediately after bathing while skin is still slightly damp. Topical corticosteroids prescribed by a doctor can reduce inflammation during flare-ups.

psoriasis.duration: Chronic condition. Flares can last weeks to months. Remission periods vary — from weeks to years.
precautions: Keep skin well-moisturized to prevent dryness and cracking
Avoid skin injuries, cuts, or infections (can trigger flares)
Limit alcohol consumption and avoid smoking
Manage stress through yoga, meditation, or regular exercise
Get moderate sun exposure (10–15 mins daily) but avoid sunburn
Use lukewarm (not hot) water for bathing
Inform your doctor about all medications you take
psoriasis.tips: Coal tar shampoos help scalp psoriasis. Phototherapy (light treatment) under medical supervision is effective for moderate to severe cases.

nail_fungus.duration: 3 to 12 months of treatment. Complete nail clearance may take 6–18 months as nails grow slowly.
precautions: Keep nails short, dry, and clean
Wear moisture-wicking socks and change them regularly
Use antifungal powder or spray in shoes
Avoid walking barefoot in public areas (pools, gyms, locker rooms)
Do not share nail clippers, files, or other nail tools
Wear well-ventilated shoes that allow airflow
Disinfect old shoes or replace them during treatment
nail_fungus.tips: Over-the-counter antifungal nail lacquers can help mild cases. For severe infections, oral antifungal medications prescribed by a doctor are more effective.
